results.rendered_html: rāja—śekhara m. (also with kavi, sūri &c.) N. of various authors and teachers; (esp.) of a poet (son of Durdaka and Śīla-vatī, tutor of Mahendrapāla, king of Kānyakubja; he lived in the 10th century and wrote 4 plays, viz. Pracaṇḍa-pāṇḍava or Bāla-bhārata, Bāla-rāmāyaṇa, Viddhaśāla-bhañjikā, and Karpūra-mañjarī)

results.rendered_html: rājaśekhara Author of the Bāla-Rāmāyaṇa, Bāla-Bhārata, and Viddhaśālabhañjikā. He lived after Bhavabhūti and before the end of the tenth century, that is, he flourished between the end of the 7th and the middle of the 10th century.
